channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Agriculture Drives South Africa's Economic Growth in 2025
item.description: The latest Statistics South Africa GDP figures have highlighted the agricultural sector as a key driver of economic stability. South Africa's GDP grew by 0.4% in the final quarter of 2025, resulting i...

item.title: Troops successfully defend Goniri military base against ISWAP attack
item.description: Troops from Sector 2 of the Joint Task Force, Northeast Operation HADIN KAI, successfully defended their position at the 120 Task Force Battalion in Goniri, Gujba LGA, Yobe State from a coordinated at...

item.title: Israel Conducts Airstrikes on Hezbollah Positions in Lebanon
item.description: Israel has launched a series of airstrikes targeting Hezbollah infrastructure in Lebanon amid escalating tensions in the region. The strikes, part of a broader response to ongoing threats, hit command...

item.title: How Digital Identity Orchestration Simplifies Identity Verification
item.description: As more people dey use digital services for Nigeria and worldwide, companies dey face wahala managing multiple identity systems. Identity orchestration na process wey dey link different identity syste...

item.title: UN envoy starts talks with Tanzanian political parties on governance and democracy
item.description: A United Nations (UN) delegation, led by Parfait Onanga-Anyanga, has started consultations with Tanzanian political parties to discuss democracy, governance, and national unity. His visit has received...

item.title: Omah Lay Talks About Afrobeats Origins and Lagos’ Role, Sparks Mixed Opinions
item.description: Omah Lay don create gbege online after him talk say Afrobeats na mainly Yoruba and say Lagos na the centre of the music genre. The singer yarn this one for London listening party ahead of him third al...

item.title: The Role of Silence as a Tool of Political Influence
item.description: In politics, silence is far from mere inaction; it is a calculated tool wielded to maintain, signal, and exert power. Jo Tarnawsky explores how staying silent can protect interests, exclude rivals, an...

item.title: IMF Review Talks with Pakistan Postponed Due to Middle East Tensions
item.description: Islamabad has not been able to finalise a staff-level agreement (SLA) with the International Monetary Fund (IMF) for the third review of Pakistan’s $7 billion Extended Fund Facility (EFF) and the seco...

item.title: Concerns grow over passport-selling scandal as President Ruto remains silent
item.description: The ongoing passport-selling scandal in Kenya is raising serious concerns among citizens and observers alike. President William Ruto has yet to publicly address allegations, causing worry among the pu...

item.title: Ombudsman receives graft complaint against Occidental Mindoro Representative
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — A formal graft complaint has been submitted to the Office of the Ombudsman against Rep. Leody Tarriela, representing the lone district of Occidental Mindoro, for allegedly accept...

item.title: Olivia Attwood discusses personal life and social media post-separation
item.description: Olivia Attwood has openly reflected on her emotional state following her split from footballer Bradley Dack earlier this year. The couple reportedly parted ways after trust issues arose in their marri...

item.title: How smartphones are reshaping Tanzania’s digital culture and opportunities
item.description: In Tanzania, smartphone ownership has grown rapidly in recent years, transforming how people communicate, transact, and express themselves online. Once seen as luxury gadgets, smartphones have become ...

item.title: Iranian public loses faith in prospects for political reform after leadership crisis
item.description: Following a brutal crackdown on anti-regime protests in Iran in January, many Iranians, including Mandana (a pseudonym used in reporting), have abandoned hope for reform from within the Islamic Republ...

item.title: Gunfire Reported During Wedding Attended by Farooq Abdullah in J&K
item.description: In Jammu and Kashmir, a wedding event attended by senior political leader Farooq Abdullah was reportedly disrupted when shots were fired at the venue. The incident caused panic among the guests and se...

item.title: Pakistan urges immediate ceasefire in Gulf conflict, supports UN resolutions for civilian protection
item.description: Pakistan has called for an immediate ceasefire in the Middle East, stressing that only peaceful negotiations can prevent further civilian casualties and regional instability. Ambassador Asim Iftikhar ...

item.title: ASX200 falls as oil supply fears rise amid Middle East tensions
item.description: Investor concerns over the ongoing US-Israeli conflict with Iran, coupled with heightened fears about global oil supply, have pushed the Australian share market sharply lower. The S&P/ASX200 fell 1.3 ...

item.title: Yanga defender Dickson Job to miss three months due to knee surgery
item.description: Dar es Salaam: Young Africans (Yanga) and Taifa Stars defender, Dickson Job, has successfully undergone knee surgery in South Africa today, March 11, 2026, following an injury he got during the Kariak...

item.title: Chinese and Indian Carmakers Gain Ground in South Africa’s Auto Market
item.description: South Africa's automotive sector is experiencing a notable shift as Chinese and Indian carmakers expand their presence. Budget-friendly brands like Chery and Great Wall Motor have seen increased marke...

item.title: Poilievre to Visit U.S. to Highlight Canadian Trade, Auto, and Energy Interests
item.description: Federal Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre is embarking on a U.S. tour next week to promote Canada's auto and energy sectors and to advocate for tariff-free trade. The trip includes stops in Michiga...
